meta_title: For Students: email automation to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to email automation and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Email Automation to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with project ideas. Learn how to build an email automation tool using Antigravity and Claude Code in this intermediate-level student case study.
content.answer_block: The 'no project idea' trap ends when you solve your own workflow inefficiencies. By building an automated email parser and responder, you create a tangible utility that demonstrates backend logic, API integration, and database management—the exact trifecta recruiters look for in a junior developer's portfolio.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank IDE, the cursor blinking like a heartbeat, mocking your lack of a portfolio-worthy idea. It’s the classic 'student syndrome': you have the technical skills but lack the context to apply them to something that actually matters. You aren't just missing an idea; you are missing a system to turn small problems into large engineering wins.

Today, we pivot. We are going to build an automated email processing engine. This isn't just a script; it’s a production-grade utility that handles incoming data, processes it through an LLM, and triggers automated responses. This project moves you from a 'tutorial follower' to an 'AI builder' who understands how to orchestrate autonomous agents.

steps.title: Architecting the Email Pipeline
steps.description: Define the structure of your email ingestion service. We need to handle webhooks from an email provider, parse the JSON payload, and isolate the sender's intent.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a FastAPI structure for an email webhook receiver. Include pydantic models for the email body, subject, and sender headers.

steps.title: Integrating LLM Intelligence
steps.description: Connect the Claude API to analyze the incoming email text. This allows your app to categorize the email's urgency and draft a context-aware response.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a service function that takes a text string, sends it to Claude API with a system prompt to classify the intent as 'urgent', 'query', or 'spam'.

steps.title: Implementing SMTP Dispatch
steps.description: Set up the outbound email logic to send the drafted response back to the user securely using environment variables for credentials.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement an SMTP mailing function using Python's smtplib. Ensure it uses TLS and handles connection errors gracefully.

steps.title: Building the Persistence Layer
steps.description: Log every processed email into a SQLite database to track performance and history. This adds a critical 'real-world' feature to your project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Define a SQLAlchemy model for an EmailLog table, including fields for timestamp, sender, intent_category, and status.

steps.title: Deployment and Testing
steps.description: Wrap your application in a Docker container and test the end-to-end flow using a local tunneling service like ngrok.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Dockerfile that installs requirements, exposes port 8000, and runs the FastAPI application using Uvicorn.

pro_tips: Always sanitize your email headers before passing them to an LLM to prevent prompt injection attacks.
Use a 'human-in-the-loop' flag for high-priority emails so the AI drafts, but you approve before sending.
Implement rate limiting on your webhook endpoint to avoid being blacklisted by email providers during testing.
Store your environment variables in a .env file and ensure it is listed in your .gitignore to avoid leaking credentials.
Add a simple 'unsubscribe' header logic to your outgoing emails to remain compliant with CAN-SPAM regulations.
content.next_steps: Once your bot is running, deploy it to a free-tier cloud service like Railway or Render and add a public dashboard to your portfolio to showcase the logs generated by your automation.
content.conversion_block: Don't let your code sit idle on a hard drive. Turn this project into your career catalyst by documenting every hurdle you cleared. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
